task.title: Normalize whitespace noise instead of preserving it in formatted Nushell
task.summary: The formatter reliably strips trailing spaces, but it still preserves large runs of internal spaces, ragged indentation, and some statement-boundary collapses after whitespace-only damage is introduced into real fixtures.
task.problem: When the fixture corpus is intentionally dirtied with repeated spaces and inconsistent indentation, `nuparu --write` produces syntactically valid and idempotent output, but much of the whitespace noise survives. In several places it also cements bad statement joins like adjacent `print` calls or neighboring function invocations on the same line. That means the formatter is robust against lexing failures, but not yet robust against whitespace corruption in source text.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Repeated internal spaces around keywords, commands, operators, and pipelines collapse to the formatter's normal single-space style when there is no syntax-specific reason to preserve them.
Indented multiline shapes such as grouped expressions and pipelines are reindented to the configured width instead of inheriting arbitrary source indentation.
Whitespace-only noise does not reactivate statement-boundary collapse for neighboring commands like adjacent print statements or adjacent top-level invocations.
Regression tests are drawn from the real fixture failures produced by the whitespace-damage experiment, not only from synthetic minimal cases.

task.notes: The checked-in formatter regressions for internal space collapse, noisy grouped indentation, and noisy statement-boundary preservation are now green. The remaining floating fixture diffs have been split into follow-up task 0011.
